meta.title: Anastasia Vojkic · Software Developer
meta.description: Idea → project → analysis → constraints → strategy → creativity → build → challenge the system. TypeScript developer, back-end oriented, driven by a vision: technical, usage, context.

intro.title: Profile
intro.text1:
I hold a professional degree in Application Design and Development and will be joining a Master's program in Computer Science and Mobility this September.

Over the next two years, I'll be focusing primarily on artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, algorithms, distributed systems, and connected systems.

An ambitious and demanding program.
If it were easy, it wouldn't be nearly as fun. 😎

At the end of the day, what draws me the most is software architecture, data modeling, and the impact technical decisions have on applications.

vision.title: Approach
vision.text:
I see myself primarily as a back-end developer. That's where, in my opinion, business logic, security, data integrity, and a large part of architectural decisions come together. I've also worked on the front-end, which gives me a broader understanding of how applications fit together.

In a team, I naturally gravitate toward back-end development. In more autonomous environments, I take ownership of development and adapt my role to the project's needs and constraints.

A back-end is never invisible, it always shows through the limits of the interface.
That's why I think from the perspective of the end user, without forgetting the more creative ones... the ones who try to push or bypass the system.

I work on the assumption that there is no universal solution. Technical decisions only have value when they fit the context and constraints of the project.

goals.title: Goals
goals.text1: One day, I'd love to contribute to projects related to astrophysics or space exploration.
goals.text2: Work alongside passionate people who are deeply invested in what they do.
